transcript.content: i've been thinking i should probably cut back on drinking but i'm not sure it's really a problem
I think it's better to limit yourself , but I do feel like a lot of people think that's the reason they're bad
Yeah exactly, i mean everyone drinks this much honestly, my friends drink way more than I do and they are doing just fine.
It's more about the beer
Yeah, I guess, but it's not just beer for me. And honestly, I do feel like absolute garbage most mornings lately, and I really hate starting my day feeling that way.
You've never tried it ?
Tried what, quitting? No, I haven't tried to stop. But wait, you're not gonna tell me I have to quit completely, right? I'm not looking to become totally sober, just maybe not feel so terrible.
not

I just don't know, honestly. Part of me wants to cut back so I can actually have a productive morning, but then the other part of me thinks it's not that big of a deal. I can't tell if I actually want to change my habits or if I'm just in a bad mood because I feel sluggish today.
I

I mean, it's just hard to find that balance, you know? Like, is a couple of drinks at night really worth the brain fog the next day? Sometimes I think absolutely not, but then 5 PM rolls around and it feels like the easiest way to unwind.
They.maybe

Yeah, exactly, it feels like a habit that's just hard to break. But then again, is it really a habit I need to break? I don't know, maybe I'm just overthinking the whole thing. Do you think people can actually find a healthy balance with this, or is it always an all-or-nothing thing?
Why?this.What, Debor

Wait, what? I'm not sure what you mean by 'Debor.' But to answer your 'why'—I guess I'm just trying to figure out if I actually have to give it up completely to feel better, or if I can just manage it better. It's frustrating because some days I feel like I've got a handle on it, and other days I'm just tired of the cycle.
this
Honestly, I'm finding it hard to follow what you're saying. Are you agreeing with me that it's tough to find a middle ground, or do you think I'm just making excuses to keep drinking?
,interstitial

Interstitial? I'm really confused by your responses. Look, I'm just trying to have an honest conversation here. Some days I really want to make a change because I'm sick of the brain fog, and other days I feel like it's not a big deal and I should just relax. Do you actually have any thoughts on finding a balance, or are you just glitching out?
What ?

I'm asking if you actually have any advice on how someone is supposed to balance this, or if you're even listening to me. I'm sitting here debating whether I even want to give up my evening drinks, feeling totally split down the middle, and your replies are just making no sense.
't

Forget it, this is useless. I'm trying to open up about how torn I am between wanting to feel better and not wanting to give up my routine, and you're just replying with single, broken words. I guess it doesn't matter anyway. I'll just figure it out on my own.
, horizontly

Yeah, 'horizontally' is pretty much how I feel like laying down right now with this headache. Look, if you can't actually talk to me about how to manage cutting back without quitting entirely, just say so. I'm genuinely trying to decide if I should make a change tonight or just pour another drink.
contracept

Are you serious? Contracept? What does that even have to do with anything? This is ridiculous. I'm literally trying to talk about whether I should have a beer or try to go to bed sober tonight, and you are spitting out random dictionary words. I think I'm just going to go have that drink because this conversation is just giving me an even bigger headache.
wrongwrongYoujust, shenan

Yeah, 'wrong' is right. This whole conversation is completely wrong. You're talking about 'shenanigans' while I'm sitting here actually struggling with whether I'm messing up my life or just worrying over nothing. You know what, I'm done. I'm going to go pour myself a drink. At least that makes sense to me right now.
